entities.name: BARLEY
entities.description: The crop species being studied for genetic mapping and gene distribution.

entities.name: ESTS
entities.description: Expressed sequence tags used as a source for identifying microsatellite motifs.

entities.name: SSRS
entities.description: Simple sequence repeats, also known as microsatellite motifs.

entities.name: EST-SSRS
entities.description: Microsatellite loci derived from expressed sequence tags.

entities.name: GENETIC MAPPING
entities.description: The process of determining the relative positions of markers on a chromosome.

entities.name: LINKAGE GROUPS
entities.description: Groups of markers that are inherited together, representing chromosomes.

entities.name: POLYMORPHISM INFORMATION CONTENT
entities.description: A metric used to evaluate the informativeness of genetic markers.

entities.name: BARLEY GENOME
entities.description: The complete genetic sequence of the barley plant.

entities.name: BAC LIBRARY
entities.description: Bacterial artificial chromosomes library used for physical mapping of markers.

entities.name: CONTIGS
entities.description: Overlapping DNA segments that represent a continuous region of the genome.

entities.name: GENE DISTRIBUTION
entities.description: The spatial arrangement of genes within the genome, found to be non-uniform in barley.

relationships.description: EST-SSRs were placed onto a genetic map of barley
EST-SSRs were mapped using genetic mapping techniques
EST-SSRs show a uniform distribution along seven linkage groups
Used to measure the informativeness of the EST-SSR markers
Used to investigate the physical distribution of EST-SSRs
EST-SSRs are distributed within the barley genome
Some EST-SSRs are physically linked within contigs
The physical linkage of EST-SSRs provides evidence of uneven gene distribution
ESTs were searched for the presence of SSR motifs
ESTs were used to derive EST-SSR markers
